title: Landscape Fabric for Gravel Driveways and Paths
author: DOTDAY

seo.metaTitle: Landscape Fabric for Gravel | XBAR by DOTDAY
seo.metaDescription: The right landscape fabric for gravel stops rutting and weed pushback under stone. See why XBAR woven-hybrid fabric is built for gravel and high traffic.

ogImage.alt: Landscape fabric for gravel installed under a stone driveway

sections.heading: The landscape fabric built for gravel
sections.subheading: Loose stone moves, ruts, and lets weeds push back when it sits on the wrong fabric. XBAR is engineered for gravel, rock, and high-traffic ground.

sections.heading: Why gravel fails on the wrong fabric
sections.body: Gravel puts constant point load and movement on whatever sits beneath it. Light garden fabric was never built for that. It tears at the stone edges, lets fines migrate up, and weeds find the gaps.
bullets: Rutting and pumping where tires and feet concentrate load
Stone working down into soil, so you top up gravel every season
Weeds pushing through thin or punctured fabric

sections.heading: XBAR is built for ground that takes a beating
sections.body: XBAR is a 5oz woven-hybrid fabric: a woven face for tensile strength with a non-woven backing for separation. Designed for gravel, rock, pavers, and high-traffic areas where lighter fabric gives out.

cards.title: Gravel driveways
cards.body: Holds stone and soil apart under vehicle load so the surface stays put.

cards.title: Gravel paths and patios
cards.body: A stable base under foot traffic that resists rutting and weed pushback.

cards.title: Paver base
cards.body: Separation under the bedding layer so the base does not pump or migrate.

cards.title: High-traffic ground
cards.body: Built for the loads that tear lighter garden fabric over a season.

How much fabric will you need?
sections.intro: Enter your area to size your roll before you order.

items.quote: Put XBAR under a gravel drive last fall. No rutting, no weeds, no top-up this spring.
items.author: Marcus T.

items.quote: Finally a fabric that holds up under stone. The backing makes a real difference.
items.author: Dana R.

items.q: Do I need woven or non-woven fabric under gravel?
items.a: For gravel you want a strong woven or woven-hybrid fabric like XBAR. It resists the movement and point load that stone puts on the base. Non-woven TERRA is built for drainage and soil separation jobs like French drains.

items.q: Will weeds still grow through fabric under gravel?
items.a: A heavy woven-hybrid fabric like XBAR blocks the pushback that thin garden fabric lets through. Keep gravel topped up and seams overlapped so there are no gaps.

items.q: Can I use garden weed barrier under a driveway?
items.a: Light garden fabric is not built for vehicle load. It tends to rut and tear at the stone edges. Use XBAR for driveways and other high-traffic gravel.

sections.heading: Ready to gravel the right way?
sections.body: Match your ground condition to the fabric built for it. Run the numbers or talk to us about bulk pricing for your crew.
